personas.name: HR Engagement Lead (MNC)
personas.icp: HR Engagement / EX leader at SG-based MNC of 500-5,000 staff; runs annual D&D + town hall + family day; KPI = attendance and post-event NPS, not vendor revenue
pains: Repeating the same goodie-bag formula every year with no measurable engagement uplift
Procurement-heavy vendor selection eats 6+ weeks pre-event
MNC HQ pressure to demonstrate ROI on engagement events
Lucky-draw budget feels arbitrary and rarely lands in employees' top recall
personas.current_workaround: Books Pico/Bray Leino Splash for production, Giftano for vouchers, photo booth from Vivid Snaps; assembles a la carte each year

nba.summary: Current annual stack: Pico AV (S$30k) + Giftano vouchers (S$10k) + Vivid Snaps booth (S$3k) + lucky draw inventory (S$5k); flashcart replaces inventory + booth lines.

implications.headline: Anchor MNC pricing on attendance + NPS, not revenue share
implications.body: Persona is judged on attendance and post-event NPS, not on vendor revenue. Annual Engagement Partner contract should report attendance-uplift metrics in the deck, never a GMV split.

personas.name: EX / Culture Manager (mid-size SME)
personas.icp: EX/Culture/People Ops manager at 100-500 headcount SG SME; runs quarterly micro-activations and one annual D&D; budget owner for culture-building cadence
pains: Balancing quarterly activation cadence against fixed annual engagement budget
Hybrid-work attendance dropping for in-person events; needs experiential pull
No internal team to build curated supplier lists for each quarterly slot
Has to justify each quarterly spend against revenue-team budget pressure
personas.current_workaround: Knoxx or Bon Events for quarterly activations; HoneySpree hampers for ad-hoc gifting; HR team does ad-hoc supplier sourcing

nba.summary: Quarterly Bon Events package + ad-hoc HoneySpree hamper run averages S$2-3k/qtr per activation slot; flashcart Quarterly Activation Package directly replaces this slot.

implications.headline: Quarterly cadence pricing is the right structural unit for SME
implications.body: Persona budgets in quarters, not years. Lead with quarterly activation package pricing and avoid annual contracts that look like a procurement project.

personas.name: Office Manager (small SG SME)
personas.icp: Office Manager / EA-to-CEO / generalist admin at 30-100 headcount SG SME; runs the office, sources the cake, and handles the once-a-year Christmas lunch and lucky draw
pains: Wears 8 hats and engagement events are 5% of the job, not the priority
Lacks vendor relationships beyond the FairPrice + delivery florist defaults
Wants a one-call solution that handles the whole office event end-to-end
Tight budget where every line item is scrutinised by the founder/MD
personas.current_workaround: FairPrice gift cards + cake delivery + a few lucky-draw items from Lazada; sometimes Funworks for a half-day team-build

nba.summary: Office Manager spends ~5 hrs/qtr coordinating engagement events at S$30/hr loaded rate = S$150/qtr in time cost; plus S$2k materials. Time saving is the WTP driver.

implications.headline: Persona is too price-sensitive for the upper tier; treat as Single Event entry-point
implications.body: Single Event tier (free to host with rev-share) is the only economically viable entry; do not push Annual Engagement Partner here. Volume-seed the SME segment to build social proof.

personas.name: Brand Marketing Manager (sponsor side)
personas.icp: Brand/Trade Marketing Manager at FMCG / wellness / lifestyle brand; budget owner for sampling and trade-marketing activation; wants employee-channel access at MNCs without booking individual office pop-ups
pains: Office-pop-up sales cycles are slow, with each MNC requiring its own approval flow
Sampling spend is hard to attribute to ROI vs digital ad spend
Trade marketing teams are scaling back free-sampling slots; need higher-intent eyeballs
Existing mall pop-ups (Wellness Week SG type) charge S$200-475/table with low conversion intent
personas.current_workaround: Ad-hoc Wellness Week SG / mall pop-ups at S$200-475/table; sampling agencies like 4R Activation; or skip sampling and go digital-only

nba.summary: Mall pop-up table at SG MNC offices runs S$300-475 per table per day per office, with low conversion intent. Flashcart aggregates 5-15 captive employee audiences per month, vastly more efficient.

implications.headline: Brand-sponsor side is the GMV-engine, not HR side
implications.body: Persona controls the SKU-supply economics. If brand sponsors fund the inventory, HR side can be hosted free, inverting the SGD 200-475/table fair-organiser default. This is the operating wedge.

personas.name: DIY HR (skip-flashcart)
personas.icp: HR teams across MNC and SME who default to 'do what we did last year' — lucky draw + Giftano vouchers + photo booth — and don't perceive a need to switch to onsite-curated commerce
pains: Risk-averse with vendor switching; 'last year worked, why change?'
No measurable benchmark to compare alternatives against
Procurement friction makes adding a new vendor expensive
Founder/CFO scrutiny on any new line item without a proven uplift case
personas.current_workaround: Lucky draw + Giftano vouchers + photo booth assembled internally; status-quo pattern repeated each year

nba.summary: Status-quo NBA: switching cost dominates price. Persona perceives no time saving (DIY pattern is already routine) so monthly_sgd_equivalent is 0; deviation is intentional — price is not the lever, social proof is.

implications.headline: Win-loss line: prove attendance/sales lift on first event or lose the deal
implications.body: Persona is the largest competitor by mass. First flashcart event must produce a measurable uplift case study or the relationship dies. Year-1 sales motion must include free-pilot offer for credible MNC logos.

pricing_models.name: Free-to-host with brand-funded rev-share
pricing_models.rationale: Inverts the SGD 200-475/table fair-organiser default; HR pays nothing, supplier brand funds the slot, flashcart takes margin on GMV. Lowest buyer friction, scales on slot count.

pricing_models.name: Flat per-event activation fee
pricing_models.rationale: Familiar procurement shape — looks like a Bon Events / Funworks line item. Easier for procurement to approve; loses the 'free to host' wedge though.

pricing_models.name: Subscription / annual engagement-partner contract
pricing_models.rationale: Recurring revenue that locks in MNC budget for the year; bundles quarterly activations + annual D&D goodie replacement + lucky-draw curation.

pricing_models.name: Hybrid: annual base + per-event GMV rev-share
pricing_models.rationale: Covers operating cost predictably (the base) while preserving upside as activation cadence and basket size grow (the share). Most defensible commercial shape long-term.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: MNC engagement-spend envelope is S$50-150k/yr per company; Annual Engagement Partner at S$12k is sub-15% of envelope, so price is not the binding constraint. Internal stakeholder approval is.
Quarterly cadence anchored at S$2-3k/qtr by Bon Events / Knoxx benchmarks; every S$1k increase above S$2.5k sheds materially more deals.
Tight budgets, founder/MD scrutiny on each line item; only the free-to-host tier converts at scale. Anything above S$1k/event materially shrinks the pool.
Sampling/trade-marketing budgets are healthy; brand sponsors compare vs S$300-475/mall-table benchmark and accept S$2-4k for a curated MNC employee audience as efficient ROAS.
Switching cost dominates price; persona will only switch on demonstrated uplift not on price. Free first event is the conversion lever, not discount.

recommended_tiers.name: Single Event
what_in: One flashcart pop-up at host's office or D&D
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Free to host. The brand sponsor pays. You bring the people, we bring the catalogue.

recommended_tiers.name: Quarterly Activation Package
what_in: Four pop-ups per year (one per quarter)
Curated 50-80 SKU drop per event with seasonal rotation
Brand-sponsor co-marketing within 1 dedicated brand per quarter
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$2,500 per quarter — less than one Bon Events package, four times a year.

recommended_tiers.name: Annual Engagement Partner
what_in: 12 activation slots/yr (monthly + annual D&D + family day)
Curated 100+ SKU drop catalogue per quarter
Quarterly attendance + NPS + GMV reporting deck
Dedicated activation manager + MNC HR concierge
PayNow QR + Stripe terminal fallback for international staff
what_excluded: Brand-sponsor exclusivity rights (sold separately to brand sponsors)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Less than 15% of your annual engagement-events envelope. Replaces 4 vendor lines with one partner.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered SME (under 200 staff or under S$100M turnover), pre-approved IMDA solution; flashcart not currently PSG-listed but pathway exists for the SaaS-side admin tooling

grants.name: Workplace Health Grant (HPB)
grants.eligibility: SG SMEs running structured workplace wellness activities (onsite health screening, mental wellness, structured wellness fairs); claimable on the wellness-fair line of the activation
